name: Fuengirola – circular tour of its villages
name_original: Fuengirola – vuelta circular por sus pueblos
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Mijas offer varied terrain, from challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra de Mijas to scenic coastal paths along the Mediterranean. The region features well-paved roads that navigate significant elevation changes, providing panoramic vistas. Mijas Pueblo, a whitewashed village, serves as a notable landmark and starting point for many routes. This diverse landscape caters to various skill levels, combining mountain and coastal cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Mijas The most popular road cycling route is Fuengirola – circular tour of its villages, a 26.8 miles (43.1 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 20 minutes to complete. This route explores the local villages with varied gradients. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Fuengirola – Mijas loop from Av. Vientos del Sur, a difficult 33.0 miles (53.1 km) path. This route includes significant elevation changes, offering views over the coast and mountains. Local road cyclists also love the Elephant Roundabout – Playa de Fuengirola loop from Fuengirola, a 22.9 miles (36.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Road cycling around Mijas is defined by its dramatic mountain ranges, extensive coastal stretches, and charming whitewashed villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate coastal rides to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Mijas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Mijas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Mijas?
A: Mijas offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 25 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Mijas?
A: Mijas provides a diverse road cycling experience, featuring well-paved roads that navigate both challenging mountain ascents and scenic coastal paths. You can expect significant elevation changes in the mountains, leading to panoramic vistas, or opt for flatter, picturesque rides along the Mediterranean coastline. A notable challenge for experienced cyclists is the steep ascent to the Mijas Repetidoras, known for gradients exceeding 10%.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Mijas?
A: Yes, Mijas has several road cycling routes suitable for beginners. There are 4 easy routes available, offering a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful scenery. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33959521" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elephant Roundabout – Playa de Fuengirola loop from Fuengirola&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate trail that leads through coastal areas, providing a pleasant ride.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes in Mijas?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Mijas offers 6 difficult routes with significant elevation changes. The Sierra de Mijas mountain range provides demanding climbs and rewarding views. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10286452" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Fuengirola – Mijas loop from Av. Vientos del Sur&lt;/a&gt;, which includes substantial ascents and offers views over the coast and mountains.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Mijas?
A: Mijas benefits from a climate conducive to year-round outdoor pursuits, making it an excellent destination for road cycling throughout the year. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints along the road cycling routes in Mijas?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Mijas offer breathtaking panoramic views. Mijas Pueblo itself is a picturesque starting point with stunning Mediterranean views. Other notable viewpoints include those from Pico Mijas, Mirador del Compás, and the Fortress Walls Gardens. You can also find specific viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/892243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corzo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6110876" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Majano - La Esperanza&lt;/a&gt; along various trails.

Q: What natural attractions can I see while road cycling in Mijas?
A: While cycling in Mijas, you'll encounter diverse natural beauty. The Sierra de Mijas mountain range is a dominant feature, offering views of its Mediterranean ecosystem with pine wood, carobs, and wild olive trees. For a unique natural highlight, consider routes that pass near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4498964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranco Blanco Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, known for its crystal-clear waters and lush vegetation, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637342" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarapalos Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Mijas pass through or near charming villages and coastal areas where you can find cafes, restaurants, and beach bars. Mijas Pueblo is a popular stopover, and the La Cala de Mijas Coastal Walk offers plenty of refreshment options. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937190578/fuengirola-circular-tour-of-its-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuengirola – circular tour of its villages&lt;/a&gt; will take you through local communities with amenities.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Mijas?
A: Parking is generally available in Mijas, particularly in larger towns like Mijas Pueblo and Fuengirola, which serve as common starting points for cycling routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations and availability, especially during peak seasons.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes in Mijas using public transport?
A: Yes, Mijas is reasonably well-connected by public transport, particularly buses, which can help you access various starting points for road cycling routes. Major towns like Fuengirola and Mijas Pueblo are key hubs for public transport, allowing for convenient access to the surrounding cycling network.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Mijas?
A: Mijas offers several excellent circular road cycling routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937190578/fuengirola-circular-tour-of-its-villages" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuengirola – circular tour of its villages&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 26.8-mile loop exploring local communities. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33953835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road with Mountain View – No traffic road loop from Pueblo Mediterráneo&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 46.5-mile route.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Mijas?
A: The road cycling experience in Mijas is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the region's dramatic mountain ranges, extensive coastal stretches, and charming whitewashed villages. The varied terrain, from challenging ascents to scenic coastal rides, is also a significant highlight for many.
tips: Central point of Benalmádena town, with a statue of the Benalmádena girl. Nice and picturesque, traditional place.

The stupa with a wonderful view of the sea is one of the top attractions. Much on the Costa del Sol is illuminated at night, so a visit at night is also worthwhile.
The stupa can be visited and there is no entry fee. There is also a cafe and a small shop. Meditations and spiritual meetings take place in the stupa.
Beautiful view. However, most of the photos are not from the marked location, but rather show the transmission towers at the end of the nearby driveway.

The town of Mijas is located in the middle of the Sierra de Mijas belonging to the terms of the Costa del Sol. Mijas coast can be distinguished very close to the town of Fuengirola and Mijas town, this is located about 15 km from the coast.
